Title: A Blast-Resistant NLR Gene Confers Drought Resistance by Competitively Interacting with an E3 Ligase to Protect Phenylalanine Ammonia-Lyase in Rice

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Entry step supplying lignin and flavonoid branches
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contribute to drought resistance and blast resistance; lignin strengthens tissues and flavonoids function as protective secondary metabolites under stress
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed at seedling stage; among PAL family members OsPAL1 is one of the highest expressed
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Deaminates L-phenylalanine to trans-cinnamic acid, initiating phenylpropanoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for lignin and flavonoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Rate-limiting enzyme in phenylpropanoid biosynthesis pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates drought resistance; increased OsPAL1 activity/stability promotes lignin and flavonoid accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsPAL1 physically interacts with PibH8. 2. OsPAL1 knockout reduces drought survival, while overexpression increases drought resistance in rice seedlings. 3. Genetic analysis showed PibH8 acts upstream of OsPAL1, indicating OsPAL1 is required for PibH8-mediated drought resistance. 4. OsPAL1 supports phenylpropanoid flux toward lignin and flavonoid accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; overexpression; yeast two-hybrid; BiFC; split luciferase complementation; Co-IP; pull-down assay; PAL activity measurement; genetic epistasis with PibH8-OE/ospal1
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought stress at seedling stage and panicle development stage
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The text mistakenly refers to OsPAL1 as phenylalanine aminotransferase in some places, but PAL activity assays, prior literature, and pathway context support phenylalanine ammonia-lyase function.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A useful pathway target to enhance drought-associated phenylpropanoid accumulation, but because it is post-translationally controlled by OsFBK16 and protected by PibH8, stacking with PibH8 or reducing OsFBK16 may be more effective than OsPAL1 manipulation alone.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Entry step supplying lignin and flavonoid branches
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Protective phenylpropanoid metabolites associated with stress resistance
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed at seedling stage relative to most PAL family members except OsPAL1
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Likely deaminates L-phenylalanine to trans-cinnamic acid in phenylpropanoid entry step
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for lignin and flavonoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not directly established in this paper, but functions as PAL family entry enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsPAL5 is a close homolog of OsPAL1 with relatively high seedling expression. 2. PibH8 physically interacts with OsPAL5. 3. OsPAL5 CRISPR mutant shows reduced drought resistance. 4. PibH8 can also protect OsPAL5 by competing with OsFBK16.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic/expression analysis; CRISPR-Cas9 mutant; BiFC; split luciferase complementation; Co-IP; pull-down assay; competition assay
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: OsPAL5 was not the main mechanistic focus, but was functionally validated as another PibH8-protected PAL contributing to drought resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential auxiliary pathway target for strengthening phenylpropanoid-based stress resistance, especially in combination with PibH8-mediated protection.

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: NLR immune receptor / protein stability regulator
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: CNL-type NLR with CC, NB-ARC, and LRR domains
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promote stress adaptation, including drought resistance; associated with enhanced blast resistance
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Natural accessions; Zhonghua11 (ZH11); Huanghuazhan (HHZ); Kongyu131 (KY131); W119
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced/significantly changed under drought stress; drought-resistant Hap.1 shows higher expression than Hap.2 under drought; no major difference between NILs and controls under normal conditions
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Drought stress; natural promoter variation (vg0826989447 C>T) defining Hap.1/Hap.2
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Competitive protein binding that protects PAL proteins from E3 ligase-mediated degradation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Stabilizes OsPAL1 and OsPAL5 proteins indirectly by inhibiting their interaction with OsFBK16, thereby enhancing PAL activity and phenylpropanoid accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Phenylpropanoid biosynthesis through post-translational control of PAL stability
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms competitive interaction module with OsPAL1/OsPAL5 and OsFBK16
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates lignin and flavonoid accumulation and improves drought resistance; also enhances blast resistance
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: A key upstream positive regulator that protects rate-limiting PAL enzymes, thereby enabling accumulation of drought-protective phenylpropanoids
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GWAS and candidate-gene association identified PibH8 as the causal gene at qRDR8 for drought resistance. 2. CRISPR knockout reduces drought and blast resistance, whereas overexpression enhances both traits. 3. PibH8 physically interacts with OsPAL1/OsPAL5 and OsFBK16. 4. PibH8 competitively blocks OsFBK16-mediated degradation of OsPAL1, increasing PAL activity, lignin, and flavonoid accumulation. 5. PibH8 acts genetically upstream of OsPAL1. 6. A promoter SNP creates superior Hap.1 with higher drought-induced expression and strong breeding value.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS; candidate gene association mapping; promoter luciferase assay; CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; overexpression; complementation with Hap.1/Hap.2; NIL introgression; yeast two-hybrid screen; BiFC; split luciferase complementation; Co-IP; pull-down assay; semi-in vitro ubiquitination assay; PAL/lignin/flavonoid measurements; epistasis with ospal1
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought stress at seedling and panicle stages; field drought; blast fungus infection
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter haplotypes of PibH8 for association/luciferase assays; 35S promoter in transient interaction assays; overexpression constructs in pCAMBIA1301U promoter details not fully stated
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as a homolog of the blast resistance gene Pib. Superior Hap.1 improves both drought and blast resistance in elite cultivars without obvious agronomic penalty under normal conditions.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value breeding target for multi-stress resistance. Introgression or editing of the superior promoter haplotype (Hap.1 / vg0826989447-linked allele) can improve both drought and blast resistance, especially in elite varieties like Kongyu131. Suitable for pyramiding with other drought/blast resistance alleles and for promoter editing to raise stress-inducible expression while minimizing normal-condition penalties.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No significant differences in major agronomic traits under normal growth conditions were observed in NILs; potential tradeoffs not evident in this study.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: GWAS using Rice-VarMap v2.0 SNP dataset (7,367,740 variants); RNA-seq of drought-stressed leaf samples; accession NA

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Protective stress-related secondary metabolites whose accumulation contributes to drought and blast resistance
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Drought conditions enhance its ubiquitination activity toward PibH8
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes degradation of OsPAL1/OsPAL5 and PibH8, thereby decreasing PAL activity and downstream phenylpropanoid accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Post-translational negative control of phenylpropanoid biosynthesis via enzyme stability
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Acts in competitive interaction network with PibH8 and PAL proteins
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negatively regulates drought resistance by reducing PAL activity; overexpression lowers survival under drought and knockout enhances drought resistance
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: A major negative regulator controlling the stability of the key entry enzyme OsPAL1 and the upstream protector PibH8
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsFBK16 physically interacts with PibH8 and promotes its degradation via ubiquitination. 2. OsFBK16 knockout enhances drought resistance, whereas overexpression reduces drought survival. 3. OsFBK16 overexpression decreases PAL activity. 4. PibH8 competitively interferes with the OsFBK16-OsPAL1 interaction, relieving OsFBK16-mediated OsPAL1 degradation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; overexpression; BiFC; split luciferase complementation; Co-IP; pull-down assay; co-transformation degradation assays; semi-in vitro ubiquitination assay; PAL activity measurement
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter in transient assays; overexpression construct details otherwise NA
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously known as a negative regulator of blast resistance through PAL degradation; this study extends its function to drought resistance and shows it also targets PibH8.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A promising negative target for editing or downregulation to increase phenylpropanoid accumulation and drought/blast resistance, potentially in combination with PibH8 Hap.1 deployment. Care is needed because broad E3 ligase perturbation may affect additional substrates.
